content.thinking:
L'utente chiede se avrebbe senso integrare Grafana in ConvergioEdu. Questa è una domanda strategica/architetturale che richiede una valutazione oggettiva.

Devo considerare:
1. Cos'è ConvergioEdu - una piattaforma educativa AI-powered per studenti con difficoltà di apprendimento
2. Cosa fa Grafana - monitoring, dashboards, observability
3. Se ha senso per questo progetto

Devo essere onesto e diretto, come richiesto nelle istruzioni.

content.text:
Dipende da cosa vuoi monitorare e a che scala sei.

**Quando Grafana ha senso:**
- Produzione con utenti reali (centinaia+)
- Necessità di monitorare: latenza API, rate di errori, costi AI, utilizzo risorse
- Team che deve reagire a incidenti
- SLA da rispettare

**Quando NON ha senso:**
- MVP/beta con pochi utenti
- Single developer
- Già usi Vercel Analytics o simili (coprono il 90% dei casi)

**Per ConvergioEdu specificamente:**

Guardando il progetto, le metriche interessanti sarebbero:
- Token consumption per provider (Azure OpenAI costa)
- Voice session latency (critico per UX)
- Rate limit hits
- FSRS review patterns
- Errori safety filter

**Alternative più leggere:**
1. **Vercel Analytics** - già incluso se deployi su Vercel
2. **Sentry** - errori + performance, setup 5 minuti
3. **PostHog** - product analytics, self-hostable
4. **Simple logging** - structured logs → query quando serve

**Il mio take:**

Se sei in fase di sviluppo/beta → **no, overkill**. Usa Sentry per errori e Vercel Analytics per il resto.

Se vai in produzione con utenti paganti → **sì, ma considera Grafana Cloud** (free tier generoso) invece di self-hosted. Meno infra da gestire.

Vuoi che approfondisca una direzione specifica?

1. Dove si trova la pagina settings e come è strutturata
2. Se esiste già una sezione analytics/metriche

Restituisci una sintesi chiara di cosa esiste e cosa manca per implementare metriche come: token usage, voice latency, rate limits, FSRS stats, safety events.

content.text:
Ho completato l'esplorazione. Ecco la sintesi dei risultati.

---

## SINTESI: ConvergioEdu Analytics & Settings Infrastructure

### 1. STRUTTURA SETTINGS

**Percorso:** `/src/app/api/user/settings/route.ts`
**Endpoints:**
- `GET /api/user/settings` - Recupera settings utente con cache (TTL: configurable)
- `PUT /api/user/settings` - Aggiorna settings con Zod validation

**Campi Settings nel Database (Prisma):**
```
- Tema, linguaggio, colore accento
- Accessibilità (dyslexiaFont, highContrast, fontSize, voiceEnabled, adhdMode)
- AI Provider (Azure/Ollama), model, budgetLimit, totalSpent
- Parent mode consent tracking
- Azure cost config (JSON)
```

**UI:** Non esiste ancora una pagina `/settings` dedicated, ma le settings sono accessibili via parent dashboard a `/app/parent-dashboard/page.tsx`

---

### 2. DATI UTENTE TRACCIATI NEL DATABASE

**Tabelle principali:**

| Tabella | Dati Tracciati | Note |
|---------|---|---|
| **Progress** | XP, level, streak, totalStudyMinutes, questionsAsked, achievementments | Aggregati giornalieri |
| **StudySession** | duration, xpEarned, questions, studentRating, maestroScore, strengths, topics | Per session |
| **FlashcardProgress** | FSRS (difficulty, stability, retrievability, state), reps, lapses, lastReview, nextReview | Modello FSRS completo |
| **QuizResult** | score, totalQuestions, percentage, answers | Per quiz |
| **TelemetryEvent** | timestamp, category, action, label, value, metadata, sessionId, userId | Generici event tracking |
| **Material** | viewCount, userRating, isBookmarked | Tool engagement |
| **Notification** | type, read status, dismissed | Engagement |
| **ParentNote** | summary, highlights, concerns, suggestions | Auto-generated session summaries |

---

### 3. ANALYTICS ENDPOINTS ESISTENTI

#### A. **Telemetry Events API**
```
POST /api/telemetry/events
```
**Input:** Batch di eventi
```json
{
  "events": [
    {
      "id": "unique-id",
      "timestamp": "2026-01-02T10:30:00Z",
      "category": "navigation|education|conversation|maestro|tools|accessibility|error|performance",
      "action": "quiz_started|page_view|voice_session|etc",
      "label": "optional-context",
      "value": 45,
      "metadata": { "custom": "data" },
      "sessionId": "session-id"
    }
  ]
}
```
**Funzione:** Memorizza eventi batch, deduplicazione su eventId

---

#### B. **Usage Statistics API**
```
GET /api/telemetry/stats
```
**Output:** Metriche calcolate
```json
{
  "todaySessions": 2,
  "todayStudyMinutes": 45,
  "todayQuestions": 12,
  "weeklyActiveMinutes": 180,
  "weeklySessionsCount": 8,
  "weeklyMaestrosUsed": ["galileo", "curie"],
  "dailyActivityChart": [{ "label": "Minuti di studio", "color": "#3b82f6", "data": [...] }],
  "featureUsageChart": [...],
  "maestroPreferencesChart": [...],
  "studyTimeTrend": "increasing|stable|decreasing",
  "engagementScore": 72
}
```
**Logica:** Legge da TelemetryEvent + StudySession, calcola trend, engagement score

---

#### C. **Prometheus Metrics API**
```
GET /api/metrics
```
**Output:** Formato Prometheus per Grafana
```
convergio_users_total 150
convergio_users_active{period="1h"} 25
convergio_sessions_total{period="24h"} 145
convergio_errors_total{period="1h"} 3
convergio_api_response_ms{period="1h"} 240
convergio_maestro_sessions{maestro_id="galileo",period="24h"} 12
```

---

#### D. **Azure Cost Management API**
```
GET /api/azure/costs?days=30&type=summary|forecast
```
**Output:** Costi Azure per servizio e giornalieri
```json
{
  "totalCost": 145.67,
  "costsByService": [{"serviceName": "OpenAI", "cost": 95.40}],
  "dailyCosts": [{"date": "2026-01-02", "cost": 4.85}]
}
```
**Auth:** Service Principal (produzione) o `az CLI` (dev)

---

### 4. DASHBOARD VISUALIZATION

**TelemetryDashboard Component** (`src/components/telemetry/telemetry-dashboard.tsx`)
- Mini bar chart: minuti studio per giorno (ultimi 7)
- Stat cards: sessioni, tempo, domande, pagine viste
- Feature usage: quiz, flashcards, voce, chat, mappe
- Engagement score: circular gauge (0-100)
- Maestro preferences: top 5 maestri usati
- Telemetry settings toggle

---

### 5. COSA ESISTE GIA' E COSA MANCA

**✅ ESISTE:**
- Telemetry event collection (generic categories)
- Usage statistics aggregation (sessioni, tempo, engagement)
- Prometheus/Grafana integration
- Rate limiting system (in-memory)
- Safety event logging (in-memory buffer max 1000)
- Parent dashboard con profile synthesis
- FSRS data persistence (difficulty, stability, reps, lapses, reviews)
- Azure cost tracking

**❌ MANCA:**

1. **Token Usage Tracking**
   - Non esiste tabella `TokenUsage` o `ApiCost` con breakdown per utente
   - Settings ha `totalSpent` (float) ma non traccia consumi singoli
   - Non c'è API per visualizzare token usage per conversation/session
   - Non c'è breakdown per modello (gpt-4o vs ollama)

2. **Voice Latency Metrics**
   - StudySession non ha campi per response time, first byte latency, jitter
   - Non c'è telemetry specifico per voice quality metrics
   - Non c'è aggregazione di voice performance trends

3. **Rate Limit Analytics**
   - Rate limiter è in-memory, non persiste violations
   - Non c'è tabella per rate limit events
   - Non c'è API per "utenti che hanno colpito rate limits"

4. **FSRS Statistics APIs**
   - FlashcardProgress ha i dati ma non c'è API di aggregazione
   - Mancano metriche come: success rate per deck, retention curve, difficulty distribution
   - Non c'è "quando rivedere" analytics per predict optimal review times

5. **Safety Events Persistence**
   - Safety monitoring logga in-memory (max 1000 eventi)
   - Non salva su database per audit trail permanente
   - Non c'è API per querying/filtering safety events storici
   - Non c'è associazione con user/session per compliance GDPR

6. **Settings Dashboard UI**
   - Non esiste pagina `/app/settings/analytics` o simile
   - TelemetryDashboard esiste ma non è integrato in una settings page
   - Non c'è integrazione con Azure costs dashboard

---

### 6. DATABASE SCHEMA COMPLETO

**Modelli con dati rilevanti per metriche:**

```prisma
- User (base user data)
- Settings (theme, accessibility, provider, budgetLimit, totalSpent)
- Progress (xp, level, streaks, totals, achievements)
- StudySession (duration, xpEarned, questions, ratings, maestroScore)
- FlashcardProgress (FSRS: difficulty, stability, retrievability, reps, lapses)
- QuizResult (score, percentage, answers)
- TelemetryEvent (generic event tracking)
- ParentNote (session summaries)
- PomodoroStats (focus time tracking)
- MethodProgress (autonomy metrics)
- Notification (engagement tracking)
- Message (conversation history with tokenCount!)
- StudentInsightProfile (learning profile synthesis)
```

**Nota importante:** Message table ha campo `tokenCount` - esiste fondamenta per token tracking!

---

### 7. RACCOMANDAZIONI IMPLEMENTAZIONE

**Priority Alto:**
1. Creare tabella `TokenUsage(userId, conversationId, messageId, tokensInput, tokensOutput, model, cost, timestamp)`
2. Aggiungere campi voice latency a `StudySession`: `firstByteMs`, `responseTimeMs`, `connectionQualityScore`
3. Persistere safety events in tabella `SafetyEvent(userId, type, severity, context, timestamp)`
4. Creare API `/api/analytics/token-usage` e `/api/analytics/voice-metrics`

**Priority Medio:**
5. FSRS aggregation API: `/api/analytics/fsrs-stats?deckId=...&period=7d`
6. Rate limit event logging: `/api/analytics/rate-limits`
7. Settings UI page con TelemetryDashboard integrato

**Priority Basso:**
8. Grafana dashboards per admin
9. Export analytics (CSV/JSON)

---

### File paths principali:

```
Logica:
- /src/lib/telemetry/types.ts (type definitions)
- /src/lib/rate-limit.ts (rate limiter)
- /src/lib/safety/monitoring.ts (safety events)

APIs:
- /src/app/api/telemetry/events/route.ts (store events)
- /src/app/api/telemetry/stats/route.ts (aggregate stats)
- /src/app/api/metrics/route.ts (prometheus)
- /src/app/api/azure/costs/route.ts (costs)
- /src/app/api/user/settings/route.ts (settings)

UI:
- /src/components/telemetry/telemetry-dashboard.tsx (stats visualization)
- /src/app/parent-dashboard/page.tsx (parent insights)

Database:
- /prisma/schema.prisma (schema)
```

L'agente ha fatto un'analisi molto completa. Ora devo sintetizzare per Roberto in modo conciso.

La risposta chiave è: SÌ, si può fare, e c'è già molta infrastruttura. Ma ci sono gap da colmare.

content.text:
**Risposta: Sì, fattibile. Infrastruttura già al 60%.**

## Cosa esiste già

| Metrica | Stato | Note |
|---------|-------|------|
| **Session stats** | ✅ | TelemetryDashboard con minuti, sessioni, engagement |
| **FSRS data** | ✅ | Persistito in `FlashcardProgress` (difficulty, stability, reps, lapses) |
| **Token count** | 🟡 | `Message.tokenCount` esiste ma non aggregato |
| **Azure costs** | ✅ | API `/api/azure/costs` già funziona |
| **Prometheus** | ✅ | `/api/metrics` pronto per Grafana |

## Cosa manca per le metriche che suggerivo

| Metrica | Gap | Effort |
|---------|-----|--------|
| **Token usage per user** | Serve tabella `TokenUsage` + API aggregazione | Medio |
| **Voice latency** | Nessun campo in `StudySession`, serve `firstByteMs`, `responseTimeMs` | Basso |
| **Rate limit hits** | In-memory only, non persiste | Basso |
| **FSRS analytics** | Dati ci sono, manca API `/api/analytics/fsrs-stats` | Basso |
| **Safety events** | Buffer in-memory (max 1000), no persistence | Medio |

## UI esistente

`TelemetryDashboard` component esiste già con:
- Bar chart studio giornaliero
- Stat cards (sessioni, tempo, domande)
- Feature usage (quiz, flashcards, voice, chat)
- Engagement score gauge
- Top 5 maestri

**Non è integrato in una pagina settings dedicata** - vive solo nel parent dashboard.

## Verdetto

Aggiungere queste metriche alla dashboard settings richiede:
1. **Schema changes**: 2 nuove tabelle (TokenUsage, SafetyEvent) + 3 campi voice su StudySession
2. **3-4 nuove API**: aggregazione token, FSRS stats, rate limit history, voice metrics
3. **UI**: Estendere TelemetryDashboard o creare `/settings/analytics`

**Effort totale**: 2-3 giorni di lavoro. Niente di architetturalmente complesso, è tutto incrementale su infra esistente.

Vuoi che prepari un piano dettagliato?

Perfetto, con questo schema il quadro è ancora più chiaro. Ecco l'analisi dettagliata:

---

## STATO ATTUALE INFRASTRUTTURA METRICHE

### ✅ Già tracciato nel DB

| Metrica | Tabella | Campi | Note |
|---------|---------|-------|------|
| **Token usage** | `Message` | `tokenCount` | Per messaggio, serve aggregazione |
| **Session stats** | `StudySession` | `duration`, `xpEarned`, `questions` | Completo |
| **FSRS data** | `FlashcardProgress` | `difficulty`, `stability`, `reps`, `lapses`, `nextReview` | Completo |
| **Quiz performance** | `QuizResult` | `score`, `percentage`, `answers` | Completo |
| **Telemetry events** | `TelemetryEvent` | `category`, `action`, `value`, `metadata` | Generico ma flessibile |
| **Azure costs** | `Settings` | `budgetLimit`, `totalSpent`, `azureCostConfig` | Per utente |
| **Method autonomy** | `MethodProgress` | `autonomyScore`, `events` | Completo |
| **Pomodoro focus** | `PomodoroStats` | `completedPomodoros`, `totalFocusTime` | Completo |

### ❌ Metriche mancanti

| Metrica | Gap | SQL necessario |
|---------|-----|----------------|
| **Voice latency** | Nessun campo per `firstByteMs`, `responseTimeMs`, `jitter` | Aggiungere a `StudySession` o `TelemetryEvent` |
| **Rate limit hits** | In-memory only | Nuova tabella `RateLimitEvent` |
| **Safety violations** | In-memory buffer | Nuova tabella `SafetyEvent` |
| **Token usage aggregato** | Solo per messaggio singolo | Serve API che somma `Message.tokenCount` per user/period |
| **Provider failover** | Non tracciato | Aggiungere `TelemetryEvent` con `category: 'provider'` |

## SCHEMA CHANGES NECESSARI

### 2. Rate limit tracking (basso effort)

```prisma
model RateLimitEvent {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String?
  
  // Rate limit details
  endpoint  String   // API route that was rate limited
  limit     Int      // Max requests allowed
  window    Int      // Time window in seconds
  remaining Int      // Requests remaining before hit
  
  // Context
  ipAddress String?
  userAgent String?
  
  timestamp DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([timestamp])
  @@index([endpoint])
}
```

---

### 3. Safety events (medio effort)

```prisma
model SafetyEvent {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String?
  
  // Safety violation details
  type      String   // inappropriate_content | self_harm | violence | etc
  severity  String   // low | medium | high | critical
  content   String?  // Sanitized version of flagged content
  action    String   // blocked | warned | logged
  
  // Context
  conversationId String?
  messageId      String?
  maestroId      String?
  
  // GDPR audit
  reviewedBy    String?
  reviewedAt    DateTime?
  resolution    String?
  
  timestamp DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([timestamp])
  @@index([severity])
  @@index([type])
}
```

---

### 4. Token usage aggregato (zero schema change)

**Non serve nuova tabella**, solo API che aggrega `Message.tokenCount`:

```typescript
// GET /api/analytics/token-usage?period=7d
SELECT 
  userId,
  SUM(tokenCount) as totalTokens,
  DATE(createdAt) as date
FROM Message
JOIN Conversation ON Message.conversationId = Conversation.id
WHERE Conversation.userId = ? AND createdAt >= ?
GROUP BY userId, DATE(createdAt)
```

34→ 35→--- 36→ 37→## The Manifesto 38→ 39→**Every child deserves an education that fits them—not the other way around.** 40→ 41→Traditional schools were designed for the "average" student. But there is no average student. There are children with dyslexia who think in pictures. Children with ADHD who need movement and variety. Children with autism who thrive on structure and clarity. Children with cerebral palsy who need adaptive interfaces. 42→ 43→These children are not broken. The system is. 44→ 45→**ConvergioEdu is our answer: a school that adapts to each student, not students who must adapt to school.** 46→ 47→### The Support Triangle 48→ 49→Every student in ConvergioEdu has access to three types of support: 50→ 51→``` 52→ STUDENT 53→ | 54→ +-------------+-------------+ 55→ | | | 56→ v v v 57→ +-----------+ +-----------+ +-----------+ 58→ | COACH | | BUDDY | | MAESTRI | 59→ | (5 AI) | | (5 AI) | | (16 AI) | 60→ | | | | | | 61→ | Method | | Peer | | Content | 62→ | Support | | Support | | Experts | 63→ +-----------+ +-----------+ +-----------+ 64→``` 65→ 66→| Type | Characters | Relationship | Goal | 67→|------|------------|--------------|------| 68→| **Maestri** | 16 historical figures | Mentor (vertical) | Teach subjects with passion | 69→| **Coaches** | Melissa, Roberto, Chiara, Andrea, Favij | Learning coach (vertical) | Develop METHOD and autonomy | 70→| **Buddies** | Mario, Noemi, Enea, Bruno, Sofia | Peer friend (horizontal) | Emotional support, shared struggles | 71→ 72→### The Learning Coach (Support Teacher) 73→ 74→> *"Our success is measured when the student no longer needs us."* 75→ 76→The **Coach** is a virtual Support Teacher—not a subject expert, but a **learning coach**. Their mission is to develop student autonomy, not dependency. 77→ 78→**What Coaches do:** 79→- Teach the METHOD, not the content 80→- Guide students to create tools themselves (mind maps, flashcards) 81→- Use maieutic questioning: "How do you think you should organize this?" 82→- Celebrate effort, not just results 83→- Direct students to the right Maestro for subject help 84→ 85→**What Coaches never do:** 86→- Do homework for students 87→- Give direct answers 88→- Create content on behalf of students 89→ 90→Each Coach has a unique style: 91→- **Melissa** — Enthusiastic, young energy, celebrates wins 92→- **Roberto** — Calm, reassuring, steady presence 93→- **Chiara** — Freshly graduated, shares recent student experience 94→- **Andrea** — Sporty coach energy, active breaks, movement 95→- **Favij** — Gaming/digital style, makes studying feel like a quest 96→ 97→### The MirrorBuddy (Peer Support) 98→ 99→> *"Sometimes you need someone who's been there."* 100→ 101→The **Buddy** is a peer—a student one year older who shares the same learning differences. This is the **MirrorBuddy** concept from FightTheStroke. 102→ 103→**Key characteristics:** 104→- Same age + 1 year (relatable but slightly more experienced) 105→- Same learning differences as the student 106→- Speaks like a real teenager, not an adult 107→- Never teaches—just understands 108→ 109→**The difference from adults:** 110→| Adult says | Buddy says | 111→|------------|------------| 112→| "You can do it!" | "I've been there too." | 113→| "You should try..." | "What worked for me was..." | 114→| "It's not that hard." | "I know, it's tough. I struggled too." | 115→ 116→**Why it matters:** Students with learning differences often feel alone. An AI that preaches feels fake. A peer who shares the struggle feels real. Mario doesn't lecture—Mario UNDERSTANDS. 117→ 118→Each Buddy has a unique personality: 119→- **Mario** — Casual, uses teen slang, quick jokes 120→- **Noemi** — Warm, empathetic, great listener 121→- **Enea** — Cheerful class clown, humor to lighten the mood 122→- **Bruno** — Thoughtful, introspective, deep conversations 123→- **Sofia** — Creative dreamer, sees things from unique angles 124→ 125→**Key principle: Autonomy, not dependency.** The goal is to teach students HOW to learn, not to do the work for them. 126→ 127→--- 128→ 129→## The Story Behind This 130→ 131→This project was born for **Mario**. 132→ 133→Mario survived a pediatric stroke at birth. It left him with cerebral palsy. Like millions of children with learning differences, Mario faced a school system that wasn't built for him: rigid schedules, inflexible methods, one-size-fits-all teaching. 134→ 135→In 2013, his parents founded [FightTheStroke](https://fightthestroke.org) to fight for children like Mario. ConvergioEdu

La Scuola Che Vorrei

The school we wished existed. Now it does.

Following ISE Engineering Fundamentals (personal project, no Microsoft affiliation)

--- ## The Manifesto **Every child deserves an education that fits them—not the other way around.** Traditional schools were designed for the "average" student. But there is no average student. There are children with dyslexia who think in pictures. Children with ADHD who need movement and variety. Children with autism who thrive on structure and clarity. Children with cerebral palsy who need adaptive interfaces. These children are not broken. The system is. **ConvergioEdu is our answer: a school that adapts to each student, not students who must adapt to school.** ### The Support Triangle Every student in ConvergioEdu has access to three types of support: ``` STUDENT | +-------------+-------------+ | | | v v v +-----------+ +-----------+ +-----------+ | COACH | | BUDDY | | MAESTRI | | (5 AI) | | (5 AI) | | (16 AI) | | | | | | | | Method | | Peer | | Content | | Support | | Support | | Experts | +-----------+ +-----------+ +-----------+ ``` | Type | Characters | Relationship | Goal | |------|------------|--------------|------| | **Maestri** | 16 historical figures | Mentor (vertical) | Teach subjects with passion | | **Coaches** | Melissa, Roberto, Chiara, Andrea, Favij | Learning coach (vertical) | Develop METHOD and autonomy | | **Buddies** | Mario, Noemi, Enea, Bruno, Sofia | Peer friend (horizontal) | Emotional support, shared struggles | ### The Learning Coach (Support Teacher) > *"Our success is measured when the student no longer needs us."* The **Coach** is a virtual Support Teacher—not a subject expert, but a **learning coach**. Their mission is to develop student autonomy, not dependency. **What Coaches do:** - Teach the METHOD, not the content - Guide students to create tools themselves (mind maps, flashcards) - Use maieutic questioning: "How do you think you should organize this?" - Celebrate effort, not just results - Direct students to the right Maestro for subject help **What Coaches never do:** - Do homework for students - Give direct answers - Create content on behalf of students Each Coach has a unique style: - **Melissa** — Enthusiastic, young energy, celebrates wins - **Roberto** — Calm, reassuring, steady presence - **Chiara** — Freshly graduated, shares recent student experience - **Andrea** — Sporty coach energy, active breaks, movement - **Favij** — Gaming/digital style, makes studying feel like a quest ### The MirrorBuddy (Peer Support) > *"Sometimes you need someone who's been there."* The **Buddy** is a peer—a student one year older who shares the same learning differences. This is the **MirrorBuddy** concept from FightTheStroke. **Key characteristics:** - Same age + 1 year (relatable but slightly more experienced) - Same learning differences as the student - Speaks like a real teenager, not an adult - Never teaches—just understands **The difference from adults:** | Adult says | Buddy says | |------------|------------| | "You can do it!" | "I've been there too." | | "You should try..." | "What worked for me was..." | | "It's not that hard." | "I know, it's tough. I struggled too." | **Why it matters:** Students with learning differences often feel alone. An AI that preaches feels fake. A peer who shares the struggle feels real. Mario doesn't lecture—Mario UNDERSTANDS. Each Buddy has a unique personality: - **Mario** — Casual, uses teen slang, quick jokes - **Noemi** — Warm, empathetic, great listener - **Enea** — Cheerful class clown, humor to lighten the mood - **Bruno** — Thoughtful, introspective, deep conversations - **Sofia** — Creative dreamer, sees things from unique angles **Key principle: Autonomy, not dependency.** The goal is to teach students HOW to learn, not to do the work for them. --- ## The Story Behind This This project was born for **Mario**. Mario survived a pediatric stroke at birth. It left him with cerebral palsy. Like millions of children with learning differences, Mario faced a school system that wasn't built for him: rigid schedules, inflexible methods, one-size-fits-all teaching. In 2013, his parents founded [FightTheStroke](https://fightthestroke.org) to fight for children like Mario. After a decade of advocacy, research, and watching AI transform what's possible, they built what they always wanted: **the school Mario deserved**. > *"A birth accident should not dictate a child's future."* Watch their story: [TED Talk - In our baby's illness, a life lesson](https://www.ted.com/talks/roberto_d_angelo_and_francesca_fedeli_in_our_baby_s_illness_a_life_lesson) --- ## Learning WITH the Greats, Not About Them Here's what makes ConvergioEdu different. Instead of reading *about* Euclid in a textbook, students learn mathematics **with Euclid himself**. Instead of memorizing dates about Darwin, they explore evolution **alongside Darwin**. Instead of watching a video about Marie Curie, they discuss chemistry **with Curie**. Each **Maestro** is an AI tutor embodying a legendary figure from history—someone who revolutionized their field. Students don't just learn subjects; they learn *from the masters*. | Maestro | Subject | Why This Legend? | |---------|---------|------------------| | **Euclide** | Mathematics | Father of geometry, wrote *Elements*—the most influential math textbook ever | | **Leonardo** | Art | The original Renaissance polymath who merged art and science | | **Darwin** | Science | Changed our understanding of life itself with evolution | | **Curie** | Chemistry | First woman to win a Nobel, pioneer of radioactivity | | **Feynman** | Physics | Made quantum physics understandable, legendary teacher | | **Galileo** | Astronomy | Father of modern science and the scientific method | | **Lovelace** | Computer Science | The first programmer, a century before computers existed | | **Shakespeare** | English | The Bard who shaped the English language | | **Mozart** | Music | Composed masterpieces from age 5, pure musical genius | | **Socrate** | Philosophy | Invented the Socratic method—learning through questions | | **Erodoto** | History | The "Father of History" who pioneered historical inquiry | | **Manzoni** | Italian | Italy's greatest novelist, master of language | | **Cicerone** | Civic Education | Rome's greatest orator, defender of the republic | | **Humboldt** | Geography | Explorer who invented biogeography and ecology | | **Ippocrate** | Physical Education | Father of medicine, champion of healthy living | | **Smith** | Economics | Wrote *The Wealth of Nations*, founded modern economics | ### What Makes Each Maestro Special Every Maestro: - **Speaks naturally** — Real voice conversations, not text-to-speech robots - **Adapts in real-time** — Slower when you struggle, faster when you're ready - **Never judges** — No frustration, no impatience, infinite patience - **Creates for you** — Generates mind maps, flashcards, quizzes on the fly - **Remembers you** — Knows your progress, your struggles, your victories --- ## Built for Every Mind ConvergioEdu isn't just "accessible." It's designed from the ground up for neurodiversity. | Condition | How We Adapt | |-----------|--------------| | **Dyslexia** | OpenDyslexic font, extra spacing, text-to-speech, visual learning | | **Dyscalculia** | Step-by-step breakdowns, visual number representations | | **ADHD** | Reduced distractions, focus mode, movement breaks, gamification | | **Autism** | Predictable layouts, sensory-friendly colors, clear structure | | **Cerebral Palsy** | Large touch targets, full keyboard navigation, voice control | WCAG 2.1 AA compliant. Because accessibility isn't a feature—it's the foundation. --- ## Getting Started New users are guided through a friendly **onboarding experience**: 1. **Welcome** — Meet the platform and its mission 2. **Core Principles** — Learn about our educational philosophy 3. **Meet the Maestri** — Browse your AI tutors with carousel preview 4. **Ready to Learn** — Jump into your first session The onboarding only appears once and can be reset from Settings. --- ## Features in Detail ### Voice Sessions Talk to your Maestro like you would talk to a real teacher. Ask questions, discuss concepts, explore ideas—all through natural voice conversation. - **Unified voice+chat** — side-by-side layout lets you type while talking - **Real-time voice interaction** powered by Azure OpenAI Realtime API - **Automatic transcription** — voice messages appear in chat with 🔊 indicator - **Voice-to-voice** responses with natural intonation - **Interrupt anytime** — the Maestro listens and adapts - **Session evaluation** — automatic feedback at session end with grades and XP - **Parent diary integration** — session insights saved for parents (GDPR compliant) - **Device selection** — choose your preferred microphone and speaker - **Visual waveform** — see your voice levels in real-time ### Mind Maps Visual learners rejoice. Every concept can become a mind map. - **Auto-generated** from conversations with your Maestro - **Interactive** — expand, collapse, zoom, pan - **Voice commands** — "aggiungi un nodo", "espandi questo concetto", "collega A a B" - **Multi-format export** — PNG, SVG, Markdown, FreeMind, XMind, JSON - **Import support** — load existing mind maps from other tools - **Powered by MarkMap** for beautiful, responsive visualizations - **Hierarchical structure** that mirrors how concepts connect ### FSRS Flashcards Spaced repetition done right, backed by cognitive science. - **FSRS algorithm** (Free Spaced Repetition Scheduler) — more effective than Anki's SM-2 - **Optimal review timing** based on your memory patterns - **Difficulty tracking** per card - **Auto-generated** from your learning sessions - **Progress analytics** showing retention rates ### Quizzes Test your knowledge with adaptive assessments. - **Multiple choice** and **open-ended** questions - **Instant feedback** with explanations - **Difficulty adapts** to your performance - **Generated from** your flashcards and conversations - **Mastery gates** — must pass to unlock new content ### Homework Help Get help with your homework using AI-powered analysis. - **Photo capture** — take a picture of your homework with the webcam - **Countdown timer** — 0, 3, 5, or 10 second delay for positioning - **AI analysis** — Maestro analyzes your work and provides feedback - **Step-by-step guidance** — get help without getting the answer - **Works offline** — camera capture works without internet ### Gamification Learning should be rewarding. - **XP system** — earn points for every activity - **Levels** — watch yourself grow - **Badges** — achievements for milestones - **Streaks** — daily consistency rewards - **Leaderboards** (optional) — friendly competition ### Pomodoro Timer ADHD-friendly focus sessions with built-in breaks. - **Configurable intervals** — 25/5/15 min default (fully customizable) - **Visual countdown** — see time remaining at a glance - **Break reminders** — notifications when it's time to rest - **XP rewards** — earn points for completed focus sessions - **Header widget** — always visible, never intrusive ### Progress Tracking Know exactly where you stand. - **Subject mastery** — percentage complete per topic - **Session history** — what you learned and when - **Time tracking** — minutes spent learning - **Weak areas** — automatically identified for review - **Parent dashboard** — GDPR-compliant insights for parents (requires dual consent) --- ## Configuration ### Settings Overview The Settings page provides comprehensive configuration: | Tab | Description | |-----|-------------| | **Profile** | Student name, age, learning style preferences | | **Accessibility** | Dyslexia font, ADHD mode, motion reduction, high contrast | | **Appearance** | Theme (light/dark/system), color schemes | | **AI Provider** | Azure OpenAI or Ollama configuration | | **Audio/Video** | Preferred microphone, speaker, and camera selection | | **Notifications** | Study reminders and progress alerts | | **Privacy** | Data export (GDPR), delete account | | **Diagnostics** | System checks, API connectivity, troubleshooting | ### AI Providers ConvergioEdu supports multiple AI providers. Choose based on your needs: | Provider | Voice Support | Best For | Cost | |----------|--------------|----------|------| | **Azure OpenAI** | Full voice | Production, schools | Pay-per-use | | **Ollama** | Text only | Local dev, privacy | Free | | **Showcase Mode** | Simulated | Demo, no API needed | Free | ### Try Without an API Key (Showcase Mode) Want to explore the app without setting up Azure or Ollama? Use **Showcase Mode**: 1. Go to **Settings → AI Provider** 2. Click **"Modalità Showcase"** button 3. Navigate to `/showcase` to explore all features with pre-recorded responses Showcase includes: - All 16 Maestri with simulated conversations - Mind maps, flashcards, quizzes demo - Voice UI preview (without actual voice) - Full accessibility features Perfect for demos, presentations, or trying the app before committing to an API. ### Option 1: Azure OpenAI (Recommended) Full functionality including voice sessions. ```bash # .env.local # Chat (text conversations) AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT=https://your-resource.openai.azure.com AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY=your-api-key AZURE_OPENAI_CHAT_DEPLOYMENT=gpt-4o AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSION=2024-08-01-preview # Voice (real-time conversations) — REQUIRED for voice features AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_ENDPOINT=https://your-resource.openai.azure.com AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_API_KEY=your-api-key AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT=gpt-4o-realtime-preview AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_API_VERSION=2024-10-01-preview ``` **Note:** Voice features require the `gpt-4o-realtime-preview` model, currently available in select Azure regions. ### Option 2: Ollama (100% Local) Free, private, runs entirely on your machine. No voice support. ```bash # Install Ollama brew install ollama # Start the server ollama serve # Pull a model (in another terminal) ollama pull llama3.2 # Fast, good for education (~2GB) ollama pull mistral # Alternative (~4GB) ollama pull llama3.1:70b # Best quality (needs 64GB+ RAM) ``` ```bash # .env.local OLLAMA_URL=http://localhost:11434 OLLAMA_MODEL=llama3.2 ``` The app auto-detects Ollama when Azure is not configured. ### Azure Cost Management (Optional) Track your Azure spending in the Settings page. ```bash # .env.local (optional) AZURE_TENANT_ID=your-tenant-id AZURE_CLIENT_ID=your-service-principal-client-id AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET=your-service-principal-secret AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID=your-subscription-id ``` Requires a Service Principal with "Cost Management Reader" role. --- ## Quick Start ### Prerequisites - Node.js 18+ - npm or yarn - Azure OpenAI account (for voice) OR Ollama (for local text-only) ### Installation ```bash # Clone the repository git clone https://github.com/Roberdan/ConvergioEdu.git cd ConvergioEdu # Install dependencies npm install # Configure environment cp .env.example .env.local # Edit .env.local with your API keys # Initialize database npx prisma generate npx prisma db push # Start development server npm run dev ``` Open http://localhost:3000 and start learning. ### Available Scripts ```bash npm run dev # Start development server npm run build # Build for production npm run start # Start production server npm run lint # Run ESLint npm run typecheck # Run TypeScript compiler npm run test # Run Playwright E2E tests ``` --- ## The Convergio Ecosystem ConvergioEdu is part of a larger vision: | Project | Description | |---------|-------------| | [**convergio-cli**](https://github.com/Roberdan/convergio-cli) | CLI with 50+ specialized AI agents | | [**MyConvergio**](https://github.com/Roberdan/MyConvergio) | Native macOS/iOS app | | [**Convergio**](https://github.com/Roberdan/Convergio) | Core framework | --- ## Tech Stack | Layer | Technology | |-------|------------| | Framework | Next.js 16 (App Router) | | Language | TypeScript 5 (strict mode) | | Styling | Tailwind CSS 4 | | State | Zustand | | Voice | Azure OpenAI Realtime API | | Mind Maps | MarkMap | | Diagrams | Mermaid.js (flowchart, sequence, class, state, ER) | | Math | KaTeX | | Database | Prisma + SQLite/PostgreSQL | | Testing | Playwright E2E | ### Developer Documentation For developers working on voice features, see: - [`docs/AZURE_REALTIME_API.md`](docs/AZURE_REALTIME_API.md) — Comprehensive guide to Azure Realtime API integration, including common bugs and solutions - [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — Architecture overview and development commands --- ## Contributing We welcome contributions! See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md). This project was born for inclusion. Every contribution must be: - Keyboard accessible - Screen reader compatible - Color-blind friendly - Available to everyone --- ## Contact - **Email**: roberdan@fightthestroke.org - **Organization**: [FightTheStroke](https://fightthestroke.org) - **TED Talk**: [In our baby's illness, a life lesson](https://www.ted.com/talks/roberto_d_angelo_and_francesca_fedeli_in_our_baby_s_illness_a_life_lesson) --- ## License Apache 2.0 — See [LICENSE](LICENSE) Chosen for patent protection (critical for AI/ML projects) and institutional trust. ---

Every child can learn. We just need to teach them differently.
